tasks.title: GameState
tasks.description: Hold the run's state as plain data and provide a clean factory/reset.
tasks.details: Create a RefCounted class (res://scripts/game_state.gd) with fields: snake (Array[Vector2i]), direction (Vector2i), apple (Vector2i), grow_pending (int), score (int), alive (bool), bounds (Vector2i). Provide static func new_game(bounds, start_length) and func reset(bounds, start_length). This is pure data with no engine dependencies.

subtasks.title: Define GameState class structure
subtasks.description: Create game_state.gd with all state fields as typed properties.
subtasks.details: Create res://scripts/game_state.gd. Declare class_name GameState extends RefCounted. Add typed fields: snake: Array[Vector2i], direction: Vector2i, apple: Vector2i, grow_pending: int, score: int, alive: bool, bounds: Vector2i. No initialization logic yet.

subtasks.title: Implement new_game factory
subtasks.description: Add static factory function to initialize a fresh game state.
subtasks.details: Write static func new_game(bounds: Vector2i, start_length: int) -> GameState. Create instance, set bounds, alive=true, score=0, grow_pending=0, direction=Vector2i.RIGHT, apple=Vector2i(-1,-1). Initialize snake array with start_length tiles near center heading right (e.g., for 3 tiles at (12,12): [(12,12), (11,12), (10,12)]).

subtasks.title: Implement reset method
subtasks.description: Add reset function to reinitialize state in place.
subtasks.details: Write func reset(bounds: Vector2i, start_length: int) -> void. Clear snake array, then repopulate with same logic as new_game. Reset all fields: alive=true, score=0, grow_pending=0, direction=Vector2i.RIGHT, apple=Vector2i(-1,-1). This avoids reallocation on restart.

subtasks.title: Create GameState unit tests
subtasks.description: Write comprehensive test suite for GameState factory and reset.
subtasks.details: Create res://test/game_state_test.gd extending GdUnitTestSuite. Test new_game: verify bounds, start_length translates to correct snake array (head at [0], tail at end), direction=RIGHT, score=0, alive=true, apple sentinel. Test reset: create state, mutate fields, call reset, verify all fields return to initial values. Test edge cases: start_length=1, different bounds.

tasks.title: SnakeLogic
tasks.description: Own all snake movement, turning, and collision rules.
tasks.details: Create a pure/stateless logic module (res://scripts/snake_logic.gd) with static functions. Define enum StepResult { MOVED, ATE, DIED }. Implement is_valid_turn, next_head, is_lethal, advance. Key rule: tail-vacate edge case (exclude tail from self-collision when grow_pending==0). Mutates GameState in place, returns StepResult.

subtasks.title: Define SnakeLogic class and StepResult enum
subtasks.description: Create snake_logic.gd with class declaration and result enum.
subtasks.details: Create res://scripts/snake_logic.gd. Declare class_name SnakeLogic (no extends, pure static). Define enum StepResult { MOVED, ATE, DIED }. Preload GameState for type hints.

subtasks.title: Implement is_valid_turn
subtasks.description: Validate direction changes to prevent 180° reversals.
subtasks.details: Write static func is_valid_turn(current: Vector2i, next: Vector2i) -> bool. Return false if next is zero-length, not axis-aligned (both x and y non-zero), or equals -current (reversal). Return true otherwise. This is the core anti-reversal logic consumed by InputBuffer.

subtasks.title: Implement next_head helper
subtasks.description: Compute the new head position based on current direction.
subtasks.details: Write static func next_head(state: GameState) -> Vector2i. Return state.snake[0] + state.direction (head tile shifted by the direction vector).

subtasks.title: Implement is_lethal collision detection
subtasks.description: Check wall bounds and self-collision with tail-vacate awareness.
subtasks.details: Write static func is_lethal(state: GameState, head: Vector2i) -> bool. Return true if head.x < 0 or head.x >= state.bounds.x or head.y < 0 or head.y >= state.bounds.y (wall). Then check self-collision: if grow_pending > 0, check head against all snake tiles; else exclude tail (state.snake[-1]) because it will vacate. Return true if head in body (excluding vacated tail).

subtasks.title: Implement advance simulation step
subtasks.description: Execute one tick: move snake, detect collisions, handle eating.
subtasks.details: Write static func advance(state: GameState) -> int (StepResult). Call next_head(state) to get new_head. If is_lethal(state, new_head): set state.alive=false, return DIED. Else: prepend new_head to state.snake. If new_head == state.apple: increment state.score and state.grow_pending, set state.apple=Vector2i(-1,-1), return ATE. Else: if grow_pending > 0 decrement it, else pop state.snake tail. Return MOVED.

subtasks.title: Create SnakeLogic unit tests
subtasks.description: Write comprehensive test suite for all SnakeLogic functions.
subtasks.details: Create res://test/snake_logic_test.gd extending GdUnitTestSuite. Test is_valid_turn: valid cardinal directions, reject reversals, reject diagonal/zero. Test next_head: verify head + direction. Test is_lethal: wall bounds (all 4 edges), self-collision with tail-vacate (grow_pending=0 allows tail tile, >0 forbids it). Test advance: MOVED (normal step), ATE (head==apple increments score/grow_pending), DIED (wall + self), multi-step growth (grow_pending decrements without tail pop).

tasks.title: AppleLogic
tasks.description: Place the single apple on a random unoccupied tile.
tasks.details: Create a pure/stateless logic module (res://scripts/apple_logic.gd) with static spawn function. Takes GameState and RandomNumberGenerator (injected for deterministic tests). Builds free-cell list (all tiles in bounds not occupied by snake), picks one uniformly at random, assigns to state.apple, returns chosen tile. Returns sentinel Vector2i(-1,-1) if board is full.

subtasks.title: Define AppleLogic class structure
subtasks.description: Create apple_logic.gd with class declaration.
subtasks.details: Create res://scripts/apple_logic.gd. Declare class_name AppleLogic (no extends, pure static). Preload GameState for type hints.

subtasks.title: Implement spawn function
subtasks.description: Generate apple position on a random unoccupied tile.
subtasks.details: Write static func spawn(state: GameState, rng: RandomNumberGenerator) -> Vector2i. Build free_tiles array: iterate all tiles (x in 0..<bounds.x, y in 0..<bounds.y), append tile if not in state.snake. If free_tiles is empty, set state.apple=Vector2i(-1,-1) and return it (board full). Else: pick index = rng.randi_range(0, free_tiles.size()-1), set state.apple = free_tiles[index], return state.apple.

subtasks.title: Create AppleLogic unit tests
subtasks.description: Write test suite for apple spawning logic.
subtasks.details: Create res://test/apple_logic_test.gd extending GdUnitTestSuite. Test spawn with seeded RNG: verify apple is unoccupied, verify determinism (same seed produces same tile). Test board-full edge case: create state with snake filling all but one tile, verify spawn picks that tile; fill entire board, verify spawn returns sentinel Vector2i(-1,-1) and state.apple is set to sentinel. Test multiple spawns produce different tiles (with different seeds).

tasks.title: InputBuffer
tasks.description: Queue requested turns and yield at most one legal turn per tick.
tasks.details: Create a RefCounted class (res://scripts/input_buffer.gd) holding a small FIFO (Array, capped at 2). Provide push(dir) to enqueue directions (reject immediate duplicates, cap size). Provide consume(current_dir) that pops and discards entries until one satisfies SnakeLogic.is_valid_turn, returning it or current_dir if none. Provide clear().

subtasks.title: Define InputBuffer class structure
subtasks.description: Create input_buffer.gd with internal queue array.
subtasks.details: Create res://scripts/input_buffer.gd. Declare class_name InputBuffer extends RefCounted. Add private field: var _queue: Array[Vector2i] = []. Preload SnakeLogic for is_valid_turn.

subtasks.title: Implement push method
subtasks.description: Enqueue direction inputs with duplicate filtering and size cap.
subtasks.details: Write func push(dir: Vector2i) -> void. If _queue is not empty and _queue[-1] == dir, return early (reject duplicate). If _queue.size() >= 2, return early (cap reached). Else: _queue.append(dir).

subtasks.title: Implement consume method
subtasks.description: Pop and validate buffered turns against current direction.
subtasks.details: Write func consume(current_dir: Vector2i) -> Vector2i. While _queue is not empty: pop front entry (candidate = _queue.pop_front()). If SnakeLogic.is_valid_turn(current_dir, candidate): return candidate (and update current_dir for next iteration to prevent chained reversals within the buffer). If queue exhausted, return current_dir unchanged.

subtasks.title: Implement clear method
subtasks.description: Reset the buffer to empty state.
subtasks.details: Write func clear() -> void. Set _queue = [] (or _queue.clear()).

subtasks.title: Create InputBuffer unit tests
subtasks.description: Write test suite for input buffering and validation logic.
subtasks.details: Create res://test/input_buffer_test.gd extending GdUnitTestSuite. Test push: verify cap at 2, reject immediate duplicate (tail == new). Test consume: valid turn dequeued, invalid turn discarded and next tried, 180° reversal across two queued turns rejected (e.g., heading RIGHT, queue [UP, LEFT] → consume returns UP, not LEFT). Test clear. Test empty consume returns current_dir.

tasks.title: ScoreStore
tasks.description: Load and persist the best score across sessions.
tasks.details: Create a static utility class (res://scripts/score_store.gd) with load_best() -> int and save_best(score: int) -> void. Uses ConfigFile against user://snake.cfg, section [scores], key best. load_best returns 0 if file/key missing (first run). No autoload registration needed, just static funcs.

subtasks.title: Define ScoreStore class structure
subtasks.description: Create score_store.gd with class declaration.
subtasks.details: Create res://scripts/score_store.gd. Declare class_name ScoreStore (no extends, pure static). Define constants: const CONFIG_PATH = "user://snake.cfg", const SECTION = "scores", const KEY = "best".

subtasks.title: Implement load_best function
subtasks.description: Read persisted high score from config file.
subtasks.details: Write static func load_best() -> int. Create ConfigFile instance. Call load(CONFIG_PATH); if error != OK return 0 (file doesn't exist on first run). Return get_value(SECTION, KEY, 0) (default 0 if key missing).

subtasks.title: Implement save_best function
subtasks.description: Persist new high score to config file.
subtasks.details: Write static func save_best(score: int) -> void. Create ConfigFile instance. Call load(CONFIG_PATH) (ignore error if file doesn't exist). Call set_value(SECTION, KEY, score). Call save(CONFIG_PATH). No return value; errors are silent (acceptable per GDD simplicity).

tasks.title: Game
tasks.description: Orchestrate one frame/tick, own the FSM, the tick clock, input capture, and wire logic results to view/HUD/persistence.
tasks.details: Create the main node shell (res://scripts/game.gd attached to Main.tscn root). Own Timer, RandomNumberGenerator, GameState, InputBuffer. Implement FSM (enum: TITLE, PLAYING, PAUSED, GAME_OVER). Handle _unhandled_input (map InputMap actions to buffer/pause/confirm). Handle Timer.timeout via _on_tick: consume input, call SnakeLogic.advance, branch on StepResult (MOVED/ATE/DIED), update view/HUD, manage persistence. Functions: _set_state(new_state) for FSM transitions.

subtasks.title: Define Game class and FSM
subtasks.description: Create game.gd with state machine and core fields.
subtasks.details: Create res://scripts/game.gd. Declare extends Node2D. Define enum GameMode { TITLE, PLAYING, PAUSED, GAME_OVER }. Add fields: var _state: GameState, var _input_buffer: InputBuffer, var _rng: RandomNumberGenerator, var _current_mode: GameMode, var _best_score: int. Define constants: const GRID = Vector2i(25, 25), const START_LENGTH = 3, const TICK_SECONDS = 0.125. Preload all logic modules (GameState, SnakeLogic, AppleLogic, InputBuffer, ScoreStore).

subtasks.title: Add InputMap actions to project.godot
subtasks.description: Define input actions for movement, pause, and confirm.
subtasks.details: Manually edit snaketaskmaster/project.godot or use Godot editor. Add [input] section with actions: move_up (W, Up Arrow), move_down (S, Down Arrow), move_left (A, Left Arrow), move_right (D, Right Arrow), pause (Esc, P), confirm (Enter, Space). Each action maps to InputEventKey with the specified keycodes and physical=false.

subtasks.title: Implement _ready initialization
subtasks.description: Initialize game state, RNG, input buffer, load best score.
subtasks.details: Write func _ready() -> void. Create _rng = RandomNumberGenerator.new(), _input_buffer = InputBuffer.new(). Load _best_score = ScoreStore.load_best(). Get Timer child node and connect its timeout signal to _on_tick. Set _current_mode = TITLE. Do not create GameState yet (deferred to game start).

subtasks.title: Implement _unhandled_input for action mapping
subtasks.description: Capture input events and translate to game actions.
subtasks.details: Write func _unhandled_input(event: InputEvent) -> void. If _current_mode == PLAYING: check Input.is_action_just_pressed for move_up/down/left/right, map to Vector2i direction (UP=-Y, DOWN=+Y, LEFT=-X, RIGHT=+X), call _input_buffer.push(dir). If pause action: toggle PLAYING ⇄ PAUSED via _set_state. If confirm action: handle TITLE → start new game, GAME_OVER → restart, PAUSED → resume. Mark event as handled where appropriate.

subtasks.title: Implement _on_tick simulation step
subtasks.description: Execute one game tick: consume input, advance snake, handle results.
subtasks.details: Write func _on_tick() -> void. Guard: if _current_mode != PLAYING, return. Commit turn: _state.direction = _input_buffer.consume(_state.direction). Call result = SnakeLogic.advance(_state). Branch on result: MOVED → tell BoardView to refresh(). ATE → AppleLogic.spawn(_state, _rng), update HUD with new score, BoardView.flash_eat(), BoardView.refresh(). DIED → stop Timer, check if _state.score > _best_score (if so: _best_score = _state.score, ScoreStore.save_best(_best_score), flag new-best), BoardView.flash_death(), transition to GAME_OVER via _set_state.

subtasks.title: Implement _set_state FSM transition handler
subtasks.description: Manage state transitions and update Timer/UI visibility.
subtasks.details: Write func _set_state(new_mode: GameMode) -> void. Set _current_mode = new_mode. Branch on new_mode: TITLE → stop Timer, show TitleScreen, hide others. PLAYING → start Timer, hide all overlays, show ScoreHUD and BoardView. PAUSED → stop Timer, show PauseOverlay. GAME_OVER → stop Timer, show GameOverScreen with score/best/new-best flag. Each branch toggles CanvasLayer visibility for the corresponding screen nodes (references obtained via get_node or @onready).

subtasks.title: Implement new game start logic
subtasks.description: Initialize or reset GameState and begin a new run.
subtasks.details: Write func _start_new_game() -> void. If _state is null: _state = GameState.new_game(GRID, START_LENGTH). Else: _state.reset(GRID, START_LENGTH). Clear _input_buffer. Call AppleLogic.spawn(_state, _rng) for first apple. Update HUD to show score=0 and _best_score. Tell BoardView to refresh(). Call _set_state(PLAYING). This is invoked from _unhandled_input when confirm pressed in TITLE or GAME_OVER.

subtasks.title: Setup Main.tscn scene tree
subtasks.description: Attach game.gd to Main.tscn and add Timer child node.
subtasks.details: Open Main.tscn. Attach res://scripts/game.gd to the root Node2D. Add Timer child node named TickTimer, set wait_time=0.125, one_shot=false, autostart=false. Do not add other UI children yet (BoardView, HUD, screens are separate tasks). Run godot --headless --path snaketaskmaster --import after saving.

tasks.title: BoardView
tasks.description: Render the current GameState as pixel art and play eat/death flashes.
tasks.details: Create a Node2D shell (res://scripts/board_view.gd) with _draw() implementation. Preload sprite textures (floor_tile, wall_tile, player_head, player_body, food). Take a GameState reference from Game. Draw floor, border walls, snake segments (distinct head), apple via draw_texture. Provide refresh() → queue_redraw(). Provide flash_eat/flash_death for brief visual feedback (e.g., modulate or temporary overlay; implementation can be simple timer-based color tint).

subtasks.title: Define BoardView class and preload sprites
subtasks.description: Create board_view.gd and load all sprite assets.
subtasks.details: Create res://scripts/board_view.gd. Declare extends Node2D. Preload textures: const FLOOR = preload("res://sprites/floor_tile.png"), const WALL = preload("res://sprites/wall_tile.png"), const HEAD = preload("res://sprites/player_head.png"), const BODY = preload("res://sprites/player_body.png"), const FOOD = preload("res://sprites/food.png"). Define constants: const TILE_PX = 16, const BOARD_ORIGIN = Vector2(120, 40) (example offset to center 25*16=400px board in 640x480 viewport with HUD space above). Add var _state: GameState (set by Game).

subtasks.title: Implement _draw rendering
subtasks.description: Draw floor tiles, walls, snake, and apple each frame.
subtasks.details: Write func _draw() -> void. Loop over all tiles (x in 0..<_state.bounds.x, y in 0..<_state.bounds.y): draw_texture(FLOOR, BOARD_ORIGIN + Vector2(x, y) * TILE_PX). Draw border walls: loop x in -1..<bounds.x+1, y in -1..<bounds.y+1, if on edge (x=-1 or x=bounds.x or y=-1 or y=bounds.y): draw_texture(WALL, ...). Draw snake: for each tile in _state.snake, if index 0 draw HEAD else draw BODY at tile position. If _state.apple != Vector2i(-1,-1): draw_texture(FOOD, ...).

subtasks.title: Implement refresh method
subtasks.description: Trigger redraw on game state changes.
subtasks.details: Write func refresh() -> void. Call queue_redraw(). This is called by Game after each tick result.

subtasks.title: Implement flash_eat visual feedback
subtasks.description: Play brief eat animation/flash effect.
subtasks.details: Write func flash_eat() -> void. Simple implementation: set modulate = Color(1.2, 1.2, 1.2) for a bright flash, start a Timer (0.1s one_shot) that resets modulate to Color.WHITE on timeout. Alternatively: play a ColorRect overlay blink. Keep minimal for MVP.

subtasks.title: Implement flash_death visual feedback
subtasks.description: Play brief death animation/flash effect.
subtasks.details: Write func flash_death() -> void. Simple implementation: set modulate = Color(1.0, 0.5, 0.5) for a red flash, start a Timer (0.2s one_shot) that resets modulate on timeout. Keep minimal for MVP.

subtasks.title: Add BoardView to Main.tscn
subtasks.description: Instantiate BoardView as child of Game in the scene tree.
subtasks.details: Open Main.tscn. Add Node2D child to the Game root, attach res://scripts/board_view.gd. Set position to BOARD_ORIGIN if not handled in _draw offset. In game.gd, get reference to BoardView via @onready var _board_view: Node2D = $BoardView, and set _board_view._state = _state in _start_new_game. Run godot --headless --path snaketaskmaster --import after saving.

tasks.title: HUD & Screens (TitleScreen, GameOverScreen, PauseOverlay, ScoreHUD)
tasks.description: Present score/high-score and the title/game-over/pause UI; relay confirm/pause intent.
tasks.details: Create four CanvasLayer UI shells: TitleScreen (label + instructions, shown in TITLE mode), ScoreHUD (current score + best score labels, shown in PLAYING), PauseOverlay ("Paused" text, shown in PAUSED), GameOverScreen (final score, best score, "New Best!" conditional label, shown in GAME_OVER). Each has set_scores/show_new_best methods called by Game. Visibility toggled by Game._set_state.

subtasks.title: Create ScoreHUD UI
subtasks.description: Build in-game score display with current and best score labels.
subtasks.details: Create res://scripts/hud.gd extending CanvasLayer. Add Control with two Labels: @onready var _score_label: Label, @onready var _best_label: Label. Write func set_scores(score: int, best: int) -> void: _score_label.text = "Score: %d" % score, _best_label.text = "Best: %d" % best. Optionally create a .tscn (hud.tscn) with the layout, or build in code. Position labels at top of viewport (y=10, x=20 for score, x=320 for best).

subtasks.title: Create TitleScreen UI
subtasks.description: Build title screen with game name, high score, and start prompt.
subtasks.details: Create res://scripts/title_screen.gd extending CanvasLayer. Add Control with Labels: title ("SNAKE"), best score ("Best: %d"), instructions ("Press ENTER to Start"). Write func set_best(best: int) -> void to update best score label. Position centered in viewport. Initially visible=true.

subtasks.title: Create PauseOverlay UI
subtasks.description: Build pause overlay with "Paused" message and resume prompt.
subtasks.details: Create res://scripts/pause_overlay.gd extending CanvasLayer. Add Control with Label "PAUSED" and "Press ESC or ENTER to Resume" centered. Initially visible=false. No dynamic data, just static text. Toggled by Game._set_state.

subtasks.title: Create GameOverScreen UI
subtasks.description: Build game over screen showing final score, best score, and new-best indicator.
subtasks.details: Create res://scripts/game_over_screen.gd extending CanvasLayer. Add Control with Labels: "GAME OVER", @onready var _score_label: Label ("Score: %d"), @onready var _best_label: Label ("Best: %d"), @onready var _new_best_label: Label ("New Best!" initially hidden), instructions ("Press ENTER to Restart"). Write func set_scores(score: int, best: int) -> void and func show_new_best(is_best: bool) -> void to update labels and toggle _new_best_label visibility. Initially visible=false.

subtasks.title: Add all UI nodes to Main.tscn
subtasks.description: Instantiate all HUD/screen CanvasLayers in the scene tree.
subtasks.details: Open Main.tscn. Add four CanvasLayer children to Game root: ScoreHUD (attach hud.gd), TitleScreen (attach title_screen.gd), PauseOverlay (attach pause_overlay.gd), GameOverScreen (attach game_over_screen.gd). Set initial visibility: TitleScreen=true, others=false. In game.gd, get references via @onready vars. Update _set_state to toggle visibility. Update _start_new_game to call _score_hud.set_scores(0, _best_score). Update _on_tick ATE branch to call _score_hud.set_scores(_state.score, _best_score). Update _on_tick DIED branch to call _game_over_screen.set_scores and show_new_best. Run godot --headless --path snaketaskmaster --import.

tasks.title: Integration & Manual Testing
tasks.description: Wire all systems together and verify the complete vertical slice.
tasks.details: Run all unit tests (game_state_test, snake_logic_test, apple_logic_test, input_buffer_test) headlessly via gdUnit4. Fix any failures. Launch the game in Godot editor or standalone: verify title screen shows, Enter starts game, snake moves at 8 tiles/s, WASD/Arrows steer (no reversal), eating apple grows snake and increments score, hitting wall/self shows game over, restart works, high score persists across runs. This task has no code output, only verification and bug fixes.

subtasks.title: Run all unit tests headlessly
subtasks.description: Execute gdUnit4 test suite and verify all tests pass.
subtasks.details: Run: godot --headless --path snaketaskmaster -s res://addons/gdUnit4/bin/GdUnitCmdTool.gd --ignoreHeadlessMode -a test/. Verify exit code 0. Review output for any failures in game_state_test.gd, snake_logic_test.gd, apple_logic_test.gd, input_buffer_test.gd. If failures occur, fix the corresponding implementation or test, re-run until all pass.

subtasks.title: Verify title screen and game start
subtasks.description: Launch game and test title screen flow.
subtasks.details: Run game in editor or standalone (godot --path snaketaskmaster). Verify: title screen displays "SNAKE", best score (0 on first run), and "Press ENTER to Start". Press Enter. Verify: transition to PLAYING, board renders with 3-segment snake heading right, one apple, score=0 displayed, timer ticking at 8 tiles/s. Fix any crashes, missing sprites, or FSM bugs.

subtasks.title: Verify movement and input handling
subtasks.description: Test snake steering, buffer, and anti-reversal.
subtasks.details: During PLAYING: press WASD/Arrow keys to steer. Verify: snake turns on next tick, no 180° reversal allowed (e.g., heading Right, pressing Left does nothing). Test input buffering: quickly press Up then Right within one tick; verify both turns apply on successive ticks. Test pause (Esc/P): timer stops, overlay shows, resume works. Fix input mapping or buffer bugs.

subtasks.title: Verify eating and growth
subtasks.description: Test apple collision, score increment, and snake growth.
subtasks.details: Steer snake into apple. Verify: flash_eat plays, score increments by 1, snake grows by one segment on next tick (tail doesn't pop), new apple spawns on a free tile. Eat multiple apples, verify growth accumulates. Fix AppleLogic or SnakeLogic.advance bugs if behavior incorrect.

subtasks.title: Verify collision and game over
subtasks.description: Test wall and self-collision death triggers.
subtasks.details: Steer snake into wall. Verify: flash_death plays, timer stops, game over screen shows with correct final score. Press Enter to restart, verify new game starts. Then: grow snake long enough to collide with own body. Verify: same death behavior. Test tail-vacate edge case: steer snake to follow its own tail end (legal move when not growing). Fix SnakeLogic.is_lethal or Game._on_tick DIED handling if incorrect.

subtasks.title: Verify score persistence
subtasks.description: Test high score saving and loading across sessions.
subtasks.details: Play game, score points (e.g., 5 apples), trigger game over. Verify: game over screen shows score=5, best=5, "New Best!" appears. Restart game within same session, score again but less (e.g., 3). Verify: game over shows score=3, best=5, no "New Best!". Close and relaunch game. Verify: title screen shows best=5. Play and beat it (e.g., 7). Verify: new best saved, persists on next relaunch. Fix ScoreStore or Game persistence logic if incorrect.

subtasks.title: Verify rendering and pixel alignment
subtasks.description: Check visual correctness of board, snake, apple, and UI.
subtasks.details: Inspect game visually: floor and wall tiles render correctly, snake head/body sprites distinct and aligned to pixel grid, apple renders on grid, no tearing or subpixel jitter (project.godot 2d/snap settings ensure this). Verify HUD labels readable and positioned correctly. Fix BoardView._draw or BOARD_ORIGIN/TILE_PX constants if layout broken.

subtasks.title: Edge case verification pass
subtasks.description: Test known edge cases and boundary conditions.
subtasks.details: Test: snake length 1 (only head, no body), verify movement and no self-collision. Test: board nearly full (fill with a long snake), verify apple spawns on remaining tiles. Test: board completely full (edit state or play until fill), verify apple spawn returns sentinel, game continues until unavoidable self-collision. Test: first tick after game start, snake moves immediately. Fix any edge case bugs in logic modules or Game orchestration.
